differences.term1_point: Have a definite shape
differences.term2_point: Do not have a definite shape; take the shape of the container

differences.term1_point: Very small inter-molecular spaces; molecules are closely packed
differences.term2_point: Moderate inter-molecular spaces; molecules are less closely packed

differences.term1_point: Molecules only vibrate about their fixed positions
differences.term2_point: Molecules can move around within the boundary of the vessel

differences.term1_point: Very strong inter-molecular forces of attraction
differences.term2_point: Moderate inter-molecular forces of attraction

differences.term1_point: Cannot flow; are rigid
differences.term2_point: Can flow easily

differences.term1_point: Have a definite volume
differences.term2_point: Do not have a definite volume; expand to fill the entire container

differences.term1_point: Moderate inter-molecular spaces
differences.term2_point: Very large inter-molecular spaces; molecules are wide apart

differences.term1_point: Moderate inter-molecular forces of attraction
differences.term2_point: Very weak inter-molecular forces of attraction

differences.term1_point: Difficult to compress because molecules are relatively close together
differences.term2_point: Can be compressed easily because molecules are far apart with large spaces between them

differences.term1_point: Molecules move within the boundary of the vessel
differences.term2_point: Molecules move freely in all directions throughout the available space

differences.term1_point: Occurs at all temperatures below the boiling point
differences.term2_point: Occurs only at a fixed temperature called the boiling point

differences.term1_point: It is a slow process
differences.term2_point: It is a rapid process

differences.term1_point: Takes place only from the surface of the liquid
differences.term2_point: Takes place throughout the entire liquid with bubbles forming at all levels

differences.term1_point: Causes cooling of the surroundings as heat is absorbed from them
differences.term2_point: Temperature of the surroundings remains more or less constant

differences.term1_point: Solid changes into liquid
differences.term2_point: Liquid changes into solid

differences.term1_point: Heat is absorbed by the substance from the surroundings
differences.term2_point: Heat is released by the substance to the surroundings

differences.term1_point: Inter-molecular forces are overcome as molecules gain kinetic energy and move apart
differences.term2_point: Inter-molecular forces become stronger as molecules lose kinetic energy and come closer

differences.term1_point: Molecules move from fixed positions to being free to move around
differences.term2_point: Molecules move from free movement to fixed positions, forming an orderly arrangement

differences.term1_point: Force of attraction between molecules of the same substance
differences.term2_point: Force of attraction between molecules of different substances

differences.term1_point: Attraction between water molecules and other water molecules
differences.term2_point: Attraction between water molecules and glass molecules

differences.term1_point: Cohesive forces between liquid molecules create surface tension, allowing the liquid surface to behave like a stretched membrane
differences.term2_point: Adhesive forces cause liquids to stick to the surfaces of other materials, such as water wetting a glass surface

differences.term1_point: Strong cohesive forces between molecules of a liquid contribute to higher viscosity (resistance to flow)
differences.term2_point: Adhesive forces between the liquid and the container walls also affect how the liquid flows along surfaces
